authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(138) — Silver Lake (Mono Co.)
sources.quotedText: (138) Silver Lake (Mono. Co.). Last Sat. in Apr. through Nov. 15. 5 trout

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(a) — General provisions for the §7.50(b) special-regulation waters list
sources.quotedText: Unless otherwise provided, it is unlawful to possess more than one daily bag limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon — CDFW
sources.quotedText: R6 Mono Silver Lake Rainbow Trout Catchable Put and Take April-November; R6 Mono Silver Lake Rainbow Trout Sub-catchable Put and Grow August-November; R6 Mono Silver Lake Eagle Lake Trout Fingerling Put and Grow August-November; R6 Mono Silver Lake Lahontan Cutthroat Trout Fingerling Put and Grow August-September

sources.title: HomeTownLocator (GNIS-derived) — feature record for GNIS Feature ID 267249, "Silver Lake", Lake, Mono County, CA (gaz_id 267249 is absent from the carto.nationalmap.gov/arcgis geonames MapServer/7 mirror used for other files in this dataset, despite the adjacent Mono County features gaz_id 267246 "Silver Canyon" and 267247 "Silver Creek" being present in that mirror, so this coordinate was corroborated via HomeTownLocator and Wikipedia instead; corrected the file's previous coordinate (-119.1320, 37.8180), which was ~4.6km northeast of the true location, near Grant Lake/Parker Lake)
sources.quotedText: Feature Name: Silver Lake Feature Type: Physical Class: Lake County: Mono County Latitude: 37.7771263 Longitude: -119.1252749

regulations.humanSummary: Open last Saturday in April through November 15
regulations.verbatimText: Last Sat. in Apr. through Nov. 15.

parameters.counting_rule: 5 trout daily bag; per CCR §7.50(a) unless otherwise provided it is unlawful to possess more than one daily bag limit, so possession is also 5 trout.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag and possession limit

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

stockingSchedule.note: CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Silver Lake (Region 6, Mono Co.) for catchable-size Rainbow Trout under the Put and Take program, with a stated release month range of April-November. This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.

CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Silver Lake (Region 6, Mono Co.) for sub-catchable-size Rainbow Trout under the Put and Grow program, with a stated release month range of August-November. This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.

CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Silver Lake (Region 6, Mono Co.) for fingerling-size Eagle Lake Trout under the Put and Grow program, with a stated release month range of August-November. This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.

CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon lists Silver Lake (Region 6, Mono Co.) for fingerling-size Lahontan Cutthroat Trout under the Put and Grow program, with a stated release month range of August-September. This is CDFW's provisional plan for 2026, not a guaranteed annual constant.
